messages.userMessage: but do you have a strategy you can recommend
messages.hermesActualAnswer: I detected the general domain and did not reuse the previous recommendation...
messages.rootCauseHypothesis: The omitted noun 'trading' was not inherited from the immediately preceding trading topic, and the internal domain diagnostic escaped as the answer.
messages.requiredMechanismFix: Same-domain continuation inference plus a dedicated trading handler and diagnostic-output suppression.

messages.userMessage: but do you have a trading strategy you recommend
messages.hermesActualAnswer: Trading Lab recommendation: compare the existing paper/demo strategies...
messages.rootCauseHypothesis: Trading Level 4 had one generic compare-only answer and did not distinguish listing from recommendation or inspect evidence.
messages.requiredMechanismFix: Dedicated trading reasoner with evidence-aware list/recommend/status modes.

messages.userMessage: so what strategies are in the trading lab
messages.hermesActualAnswer: Trading Lab recommendation: compare the existing paper/demo strategies...
messages.rootCauseHypothesis: The pipeline did not classify the trading sub-intent and always emitted the same advice.
messages.requiredMechanismFix: Retrieve known strategy names and separate list/recommend/status sub-intents.

messages.userMessage: where did you get your last response from
messages.hermesActualAnswer: You are referring to Funding Application Prep Sprint. Here is the implementation plan...
messages.rootCauseHypothesis: The ordinal matcher treated the word 'last' in 'last response' as the last ranked business item before trace intent was recognized.
messages.requiredMechanismFix: Trace/source priority zero-after-safety and prohibit ordinal memory matching inside trace questions.

messages.hermesActualAnswer: I detected the unknown domain. Domain override applied: no.
messages.rootCauseHypothesis: The preceding source question was not marked trace_meta, so it became the most recent relevant trace and hid the trading trace.
messages.requiredMechanismFix: Dedicated trace question type and last normal/domain trace targeting.

messages.userMessage: where are you getting the answer to your questions
messages.hermesActualAnswer: I detected the general domain and did not reuse the previous recommendation...
messages.rootCauseHypothesis: Source-question variants were absent from trace classification, allowing normal Level 4 fallback.
messages.requiredMechanismFix: Semantic source/trace classifier and dedicated handler.

messages.hermesActualAnswer: I have a live AI model (openai/gpt-4o-mini...) for strategic reasoning.
messages.rootCauseHypothesis: Capability matching used the broad phrase 'are you using' for model status after the narrower Supabase pattern failed.
messages.requiredMechanismFix: Trace Supabase handler before capability matching and remove ambiguous model matcher.

messages.userMessage: so are you using strategic reasoning
messages.hermesActualAnswer: I have a live AI model (openai/gpt-4o-mini...) for strategic reasoning.
messages.rootCauseHypothesis: Strategic-reasoning wording was treated as model availability rather than route diagnostics.
messages.requiredMechanismFix: Explain local_reasoning/model_reasoning and actual model/Supabase use from trace.

messages.userMessage: what is the most money we can make of the next 30 says
messages.hermesActualAnswer: I detected the general domain and did not reuse the previous recommendation...
messages.rootCauseHypothesis: Money intent required narrow phrases and failed on natural wording/typo; the general-domain diagnostic leaked to the user.
messages.requiredMechanismFix: Broad monetization intent family, 30-day normalization, revenue reasoner, and final diagnostic guard.

summary.primaryRootCause: Trace priority, sub-intent handlers, and internal diagnostic suppression were not centralized.
